creator: raimeetravel
caption:
The Kyoto most people miss out on (& the side of town where you won’t be drowning in crowds!)

We’ve all seen the crazy surge in Japan travel the past few years, but most people stick to the same things and it’s getting TOO overcrowded. That’s why I was so honored to work with Kyoto’s tourism and showcase some places around the city that actually WANT more tourists to visit…so please add these to your itinerary! 

*Ive since been to Japan 7x and will be back again to continue showing the lesser visited places!

💎 Comment GEMS & I’ll send you my Kyoto map + guide! 

#kyoto #japantravel #kyotoguide

transcription.text: i'm back in japan for the third time and i'll definitely be coming back again but this time my goal was to get really off the beaten path so if you're headed to kyoto here's what to do. one, head to raksai bamboo park. this is different than the really popular one. you'll have this place all to yourself. there's also a museum where you can learn about bamboo production and it's easily accessible by bike or bus from kyoto. two, wander around fushimi and explore its historic sake district which has century-old breweries. they make some of japan's finest sake here because of the pure spring water in the region. you should get the flight of 18 local sakes at this spot. three, also in fushimi, go paint some traditional japanese candles at this workshop that has been crafting these beautiful candles by hand for over 125 years. four, catch a kabuki show at minamizawa theater. this is kyoto's oldest kabuki theater and it has captivating performances that blend drama, dance, elaborate costumes, and it's just an unforgettable cultural experience. and five, visit kyoto meditation center and go on a peaceful walking tour. plus, participate in a morning meditation and you'll get a beautiful delicious healthy lunch and green tea after. this was one of my favorite things we did. i felt so at peace after this meditation. and one more bonus thing, visit yoshiminadera temple. this is a serene centuries-old site with stunning hillside views and we were the only people there. i put together a kyoto mini guide for you. it includes these hidden gems and my personal map of safe spots around the city so let me know if you want it. it's linked in my bio.
